title: eriogonum ovalifolium var. nivale
intro: Eriogonum ovalifolium var. nivale, commonly known as alpine buckwheat or mountain buckwheat, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the buckwheat family. It is native to the mountainous regions of North America and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. Eriogonum ovalifolium var. nivale can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health concerns.
